{{Infobox character
| name = Tharlos, the Formless Legion
| introduced = ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow
| name = Tharlos, the Formless Legion
| aliases = Tharlos
| appears_in = The Elder Empire: Sea, The Elder Empire: Shadow
| titles = the Formless Legion
| introduced = ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow
}}

''See also: [[Tharlos, the Formless Legion Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|Tharlos, also known as the Formless [[Legion]], is a [[Great Elder]] characterized by an obsession with constant and endless change. Unlike some other [[Elders]] who seek destruction or specific knowledge, Tharlos's drive for transformation can result in the destruction of worlds as an incidental byproduct. The entity is associated with a territory that once bordered [[Othaghor]], a region marked by historical conflict between life and chaos. Tharlos's influence is manifested through the shadow-men of Urg’naut and various [[Elderspawn]], which are shapeshifting creatures that lack a stable form, often appearing as melting combinations of animals and objects. A significant relic associated with this entity is the [[Spear of Tharlos]], an ancient weapon carved from one of the Elder's bones by a companion of [[the Emperor]]. The spear is a six-foot-tall artifact with a spade-like blade that possesses its own sentience and intent, frequently attempting to reshape its surroundings or the person wielding it. The weapon is capable of altering its size and shape, such as shrinking to the dimensions of a dagger or transforming into an animal. It is currently held by Bliss, a [[Soulbound]] individual who has integrated the spear's desire for change into her own nature.}}

== Of Sea and Shadow ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Tharlos prefers a world of constant, endless change.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c28">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 28</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Tharlos is known as the Formless [[Legion]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c28" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The power of Tharlos can change a person's skin.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c28" />}}

== Of Shadow and Sea ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|Tharlos wants to burn everything to the ground.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c17">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 17</ref>}}

== Of Dawn and Darkness ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|Driven by a constant pursuit of change, Tharlos observes the transformation of a building triggered by the [[Elder barrier]] with excitement. This entity exerts an influence over Bliss's inherent unpredictability and possesses a power so vast that the destruction of the world could occur as an incidental byproduct of its existence. Although [[Kelarac]] is sometimes associated with these events, it is confirmed that [[Kelarac]] is not Tharlos.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|Tharlos might destroy the world as an incidental byproduct.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c8">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|Tharlos influences Bliss's unpredictability.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|Tharlos is excited by the transformation of the building caused by the [[Elder barrier]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Kelarac]] is not Tharlos.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c12">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|Tharlos pursues change.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c24">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 24</ref>}}

== Of Darkness and Dawn ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Erin]] was once the border of Tharlos and [[Othaghor]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c1">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Ach'magut]] explicitly states he is not Tharlos.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c9">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 9</ref>}}

== Of Kings and Killers ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|Tharlos has bones.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c2">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|Tharlos is a squirming, shifting figure that blinks into a different form every second.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c19">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|Tharlos is an unknown entity.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c23">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|Tharlos is someone whose actions Bliss compares to changing emotional states.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c23" />}}

== Of Killers and Kings ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|Tharlos is a place associated with shifting demons.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c17">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 17</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|“I have one of Tharlos’ bones in my pocket.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c2" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:Of Sea and Shadow]]
[[Category:Of Dawn and Darkness]]
[[Category:Of Kings and Killers]]
[[Category:Of Shadow and Sea]]
[[Category:Of Darkness and Dawn]]
[[Category:Of Killers and Kings]]
[[Category:Elder Empire]]
[[Category:Elder Empire characters]]
[[Category:Titled characters]]
